key_insight: A projection-based pinhole-camera coupling maps infrared temperature fields onto DIC-derived 3D material points, enabling both datasets to be expressed in the same Lagrangian frame even on curved surfaces.
engineering_relevance: This lets experimental solid-mechanics workflows combine deformation and thermal information on non-flat components without changing existing image-registration pipelines.
method: Calibrate two independently calibrated systems: stereocorrelation DIC for 3D points and infrared thermography for 2D temperature images.
Use a pinhole camera projection matrix, calibrated from a single reference-object image, to project IR measurements onto material points.
Apply radial basis function interpolation in space and time to compute temperature gradients and rates on curved surfaces.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports accurate representation of temperature fields at material points and highlights successful evaluation using the calibrated projection model.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on reliable camera-model calibration and registration; curved-surface cases may be sensitive to projection errors and interpolation choices.
paper_title: Projection-based coupling of infrared thermography and stereocorrelation-based digital image correlation
